Programmes & Initiatives

Three programmes. One integrated system.

Each programme feeds into the others — from the trained teacher to the nourished child to the food-literate family and the resilient community.

Our Journey

From one school to a movement.

Drag to explore ›

🌱

2019

The First Seed

Founded in response to alarming child malnutrition data. First nutrition session run in a Pune government school.

📚

2020

Curriculum Built

First child-friendly nutrition curriculum developed with educators and nutritionists. Piloted in 8 schools.

👩‍🏫

2021

Teacher Academy

Teacher Training Academy formally launched. First cohort of 45 teachers trained across Maharashtra and Rajasthan.

🥗

2022

Midday Meal Integration

Partnered with district education authorities. School gardens piloted in 12 schools.

🌍

2023

Scaling Across States

Expanded to 6 states. Crossed 10,000 children reached. First state government MOU signed.

📊

2024

Impact Research Published

Longitudinal impact study published. 12,000+ children reached, 340+ teachers trained, 48 partner schools.

🚀

2025–30

Phase 2: National Scale

Target: 100,000 children, 2,000 teachers, 200 schools across 12 states.

🌿

2030 →

Systems Change

A generation of food-literate children. Teachers who teach it brilliantly. Policy that mandates it.

Programme 01

School Nutrition Initiative

Our flagship programme brings nutritional literacy directly into the school curriculum. We don't add it as an afterthought — we embed it into how teachers teach, how children engage with midday meals, and how schools think about food.

Working across Maharashtra, Rajasthan, Bihar, Karnataka, Gujarat, and Andhra Pradesh, the School Nutrition Initiative has reached over 12,000 children through structured sessions taught by our trained educators.

📚 Curriculum Integration 🍱 Midday Meal Enhancement 👪 Parent Engagement 📋 M&E Framework 6 States Active
Programme Stats

Children reached
0
Partner schools
94%
Knowledge improvement
View Impact Evidence →

Programme Components

How the initiative works

📖
Classroom Sessions
Structured, age-appropriate nutrition education sessions delivered by trained teachers. Covers food groups, seasonal eating, plant-based nutrition, and the midday meal.
👩‍🏫
Teacher Training
Two-day intensive training programme equipping government school teachers with nutritional knowledge and child-friendly pedagogy.
👪
Parent Engagement
Community sessions connecting school learning to home practices — empowering families to make informed food choices.
📊
Monitoring & Evaluation
Systematic tracking of knowledge change, dietary behaviour, and programme adherence — evidence at every stage of delivery.
🗂️
School Toolkits
Designed classroom resources: nutrition cards, food group posters, seasonal eating guides, and indigenous crop references.
🤝
Volunteer Network
Trained nutrition volunteers who extend the programme's reach into communities beyond school walls.

Programme 02

Teacher Training Academy

One trained teacher reaches 30–40 children directly — and through them, 60–80 families. No other intervention in Indian public health delivers this kind of multiplier. We call the teacher our highest-leverage actor.

Our Teacher Training Academy is an intensive, practical programme. It combines nutritional science with pedagogy designed for India's classroom realities — resource constraints, multi-grade teaching, and diverse regional food cultures included.

Training Duration
2-day intensive
Teachers Trained
0
Retention Rate
94%
States Covered
3 states
  • ✓NCERT-mapped nutrition curriculum
  • ✓Residential and day-visit training formats
  • ✓District-level cascade model for scale
  • ✓Ongoing materials, resources, and support
Sponsor a Training Cohort →

Programme 03

School Garden Programme

There is something profound that happens when a child pulls a carrot from the ground they planted it in. They understand, viscerally, that food comes from earth and effort — not a packet. That understanding is more durable than any classroom lesson.

Our school garden programme creates living laboratories where botany, nutrition, responsibility, and wonder intersect. Gardens also feed directly into midday meal programmes — making the connection between growing and eating beautifully complete.

🥬 Leafy Greens 🍅 Tomatoes 🥕 Root Vegetables 🫘 Pulses 🌿 Herbs 🥦 Seasonal Produce

"Every vegetable grown in a school garden is a seed of nutritional intelligence planted in a child's mind."

Fund a School Garden →
"We are not designing a nutrition programme. We are designing a system — where every child who learns to think about food becomes a thread in a larger web of change."

Bodhi Greens Foundation — Theory of Change, 2024

⚗️ Early-stage initiatives — design and grant development phases

Emerging Work

Beyond the school — where we're headed next.

These initiatives are in early design or grant-development phases. We are seeking co-development partners, funders, and collaborators to help shape them.

🏔️
Design Phase

Himalayan Food Futures Lab

A research and demonstration lab exploring traditional Himalayan food systems — their nutritional density, climate resilience, and potential as models for sustainable mountain communities.

Traditional cropsMountain ecologyFood sovereignty

Partner conversations underway — research design in progress

🌾
Grant Under Development

Seed Sovereignty Initiative

Building community seed libraries in partnership with indigenous farming communities to preserve nutritionally-dense heritage varieties threatened by industrial monoculture.

Heritage seedsBiodiversityFarmer livelihoods

Farmer partnerships being scoped — grant application in preparation

Help shape what comes next

Want to co-develop an initiative?

We're seeking CSR partners, grant funders, university collaborators, and farmer organisations to co-build these from the ground up.
